steps.text: Establish the base rate: the shrub grows 57 cm every $m$ months, so the rate is $57/m$ cm per month.
Convert $k$ years to months: $k$ years $\times 12$ months/year $= 12k$ months.
Multiply rate by time: growth in $k$ years $= \dfrac{57}{m} \times 12k = \dfrac{684k}{m}$ centimeters.

• Choice A — $\frac{19m}{4k}$: has $m$ in the numerator and $k$ in the denominator, inverted from what we need, and the constant is wrong. Incorrect.
  • Choice B — $\frac{19k}{4m}$: equals $57k/(12m)$, not $684k/m$. Incorrect.
  • Choice C — $\frac{684m}{k}$: $m$ and $k$ are swapped relative to our result. Incorrect.
  • Choice D — $\frac{684k}{m}$: matches our derived expression exactly. That confirms D is correct.
